name: Kafka

config_fields.name: acks
config_fields.description: The number of acknowledgments the producer requires the leader to have received before considering a request complete. This controls the  durability of records that are sent.

config_fields.name: batch_size
config_fields.description: The producer will attempt to batch records together into fewer requests whenever multiple records are being sent to the same partition.

config_fields.name: bootstrap_servers
config_fields.description: A list of host/port pairs to use for establishing the initial connection to the Kafka cluster. The client will make use of all servers irrespective of which servers are specified here for bootstrapping—this list only impacts the initial hosts used to discover the full set of servers. This list should be in the form host1:port1,host2:port2,.... Since these servers are just used for the initial connection to discover the full cluster membership (which may change dynamically), this list need not contain the full set of servers (you may want more than one, though, in case a server is down).

config_fields.name: buffer_memory
config_fields.description: The total bytes of memory the producer can use to buffer records waiting to be sent to the server.

config_fields.name: client_dns_lookup
config_fields.description: Controls how the client uses DNS lookups. If set to use_all_dns_ips, connect to each returned IP address in sequence until a successful connection is established. After a disconnection, the next IP is used. Once all IPs have been used once, the client resolves the IP(s) from the hostname again. If set to resolve_canonical_bootstrap_servers_only, resolve each bootstrap address into a list of canonical names. After the bootstrap phase, this behaves the same as use_all_dns_ips. If set to default (deprecated), attempt to connect to the first IP address returned by the lookup, even if the lookup returns multiple IP addresses.

config_fields.name: client_id
config_fields.description: An ID string to pass to the server when making requests. The purpose of this is to be able to track the source of requests beyond just ip/port by allowing a logical application name to be included in server-side request logging.

config_fields.name: compression_type
config_fields.description: The compression type for all data generated by the producer.

config_fields.name: delivery_timeout_ms
config_fields.description: An upper bound on the time to report success or failure after a call to 'send()' returns.

config_fields.name: enable_idempotence
config_fields.description: When set to 'true', the producer will ensure that exactly one copy of each message is written in the stream. If 'false', producer retries due to broker failures, etc., may write duplicates of the retried message in the stream.

config_fields.name: linger_ms
config_fields.description: The producer groups together any records that arrive in between request transmissions into a single batched request.

config_fields.name: max_block_ms
config_fields.description: The configuration controls how long the KafkaProducer's send(), partitionsFor(), initTransactions(), sendOffsetsToTransaction(), commitTransaction() and abortTransaction() methods will block.

config_fields.name: max_in_flight_requests_per_connection
config_fields.description: The maximum number of unacknowledged requests the client will send on a single connection before blocking. Can be greater than 1, and the maximum value supported with idempotency is 5.

config_fields.name: max_request_size
config_fields.description: The maximum size of a request in bytes.

config_fields.name: protocol
config_fields.description: Protocol used to communicate with brokers.

config_fields.name: receive_buffer_bytes
config_fields.description: The size of the TCP receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) to use when reading data. If the value is -1, the OS default will be used.

config_fields.name: request_timeout_ms
config_fields.description: The configuration controls the maximum amount of time the client will wait for the response of a request. If the response is not received before the timeout elapses the client will resend the request if necessary or fail the request if retries are exhausted.

config_fields.name: retries
config_fields.description: Setting a value greater than zero will cause the client to resend any record whose send fails with a potentially transient error.

config_fields.name: send_buffer_bytes
config_fields.description: The size of the TCP send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) to use when sending data. If the value is -1, the OS default will be used.

config_fields.name: socket_connection_setup_timeout_max_ms
config_fields.description: The maximum amount of time the client will wait for the socket connection to be established. The connection setup timeout will increase exponentially for each consecutive connection failure up to this maximum.

config_fields.name: socket_connection_setup_timeout_ms
config_fields.description: The amount of time the client will wait for the socket connection to be established.

config_fields.name: sync_producer
config_fields.description: Wait synchronously until the record has been sent to Kafka.

config_fields.name: test_topic
config_fields.description: manual intervention needed

config_fields.name: topic_pattern
config_fields.description: Topic pattern in which the records will be sent. You can use patterns like '{namespace}' and/or '{stream}' to send the message to a specific topic based on these values. Notice that the topic name will be transformed to a standard naming convention.

name: Vectara

config_fields.name: corpus_name
config_fields.description: The Name of Corpus to load data into

config_fields.name: customer_id
config_fields.description: Your customer id as it is in the authenticaion url

config_fields.name: metadata_fields
config_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.

config_fields.name: oauth2
config_fields.description: OAuth2.0 credentials used to authenticate admin actions (creating/deleting corpora)

config_fields.name: parallelize
config_fields.description: Parallelize indexing into Vectara with multiple threads

config_fields.name: text_fields
config_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be in the section of the document. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.

config_fields.name: title_field
config_fields.description: A field that will be used to populate the `title` of each document. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.

name: Amazon Seller Partner

config_fields.name: account_type
config_fields.description: manual intervention needed

config_fields.name: app_id
config_fields.description: Your Amazon Application ID.

config_fields.name: aws_environment
config_fields.description: Select the AWS Environment.

config_fields.name: creation_requester_429_max_retries
config_fields.description: Maximum number of retry attempts when the report creation API returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests). Each retry uses exponential backoff based on the x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit header. Reduce this value to avoid exhausting rate limits on retrying requests.

config_fields.name: failed_retry_wait_time_in_seconds
config_fields.description: Time in seconds to wait before retrying a report that returned FATAL status. Amazon enforces per-report-type cooldowns after generating a report. Near-real-time FBA reports have an approximately 30-minute cooldown, daily FBA reports approximately 4 hours. The default of 1800 seconds covers the most common cooldown. Increase this value if you see repeated FATAL errors on daily FBA reports.

config_fields.name: financial_events_max_results_per_page
config_fields.description: The maximum number of results to return per page for the ListFinancialEvents stream. If the response exceeds the maximum number of transactions or 10 MB, the API returns an InvalidInput error. Lower this value if you encounter InvalidInput errors during sync. Valid range is 1-100.

config_fields.name: financial_events_step
config_fields.description:
The time window size for fetching financial events data in chunks for the ListFinancialEvents and ListFinancialEventGroups streams. Options include hourly (1H, 6H, 12H) and daily (1D, 7D, 14D, 30D, 60D, 90D, 180D) granularity.

- **Hourly step sizes (e.g., 1H, 6H)** are recommended for very high data volumes where daily windows cause pagination token expiration (TTL errors). They fetch smaller chunks per request, reducing the risk of timeouts.
- **Daily step sizes (e.g., 1D, 7D)** are better for moderate data volumes. They balance sync speed with API efficiency.
- **Larger step sizes (e.g., 30D, 180D)** are better for smaller data volumes. They fetch more data per request, speeding up syncing and reducing the number of API calls.

Select a step size that matches your data volume to optimize syncing speed and API performance.

config_fields.name: include_pii
config_fields.description: When enabled, the connector requests a Restricted Data Token (RDT) to access PII fields such as BuyerInfo and ShippingAddress in the Orders and OrderItems streams. Your Amazon SP-API developer profile must have an approved Restricted Role (Direct-to-Consumer Shipping or Tax Invoicing). If the RDT request is denied (HTTP 403), the connector falls back to the standard token automatically and PII fields remain empty.

config_fields.name: lwa_app_id
config_fields.description: Your Login with Amazon Client ID.

config_fields.name: lwa_client_secret
config_fields.description: Your Login with Amazon Client Secret.

config_fields.name: max_async_job_count
config_fields.description: The maximum number of concurrent asynchronous job requests that can be active at a time.

config_fields.name: max_done_report_age_hours
config_fields.description: When the connector finds an existing completed (DONE) report matching the same date range and marketplace, it can reuse that report instead of creating a new one. This setting controls how old (in hours) a completed report can be and still be reused. Set to 0 to always create new reports. Maximum is 72 hours.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of workers to use for the connector when syncing concurrently.

config_fields.name: period_in_days
config_fields.description: For syncs spanning a large date range, this option is used to request data in a smaller fixed window to improve sync reliability. This time window can be configured granularly by day.

config_fields.name: refresh_token
config_fields.description: The Refresh Token obtained via OAuth flow authorization.

config_fields.name: region
config_fields.description: Select the AWS Region.

config_fields.name: replication_end_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data after this date will not be replicated.

config_fields.name: replication_start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated. If start date is not provided or older than 2 years ago from today, the date 2 years ago from today will be used.

config_fields.name: report_options_list
config_fields.description: Additional information passed to reports. This varies by report type.

config_fields.name: report_stream_lookback_window_in_hours
config_fields.description: Number of hours to re-fetch for incremental report streams on each sync. Increase this value if Amazon continues updating report data after the previous sync has completed. Duplicates from the lookback window are handled by destination deduplication. Set to 0 to disable. This does not apply to GET_SALES_AND_TRAFFIC_REPORT_BY_MONTH or GET_VENDOR_SALES_REPORT, which use monthly or date-only report boundaries.

config_fields.name: sales_and_traffic_report_asin_granularity
config_fields.description: The level of ASIN granularity for the Sales and Traffic report streams. PARENT returns data aggregated at the parent ASIN level. CHILD returns data at the child ASIN level with populated childAsin values. SKU returns data at the individual SKU level with populated childAsin and sku values.

config_fields.name: stop_sync_on_rate_limit
config_fields.description: Only applies to report streams. When enabled, the source stops retrying immediately once the rate limit retry budget is exhausted and fails with an actionable configuration error. This is useful when persistent rate limiting indicates the connector configuration needs adjustment (such as reducing the number of report streams or increasing the sync interval). When disabled (default), the source applies its backoff and retry strategy; if all retry attempts are exhausted, a transient error is thrown and the sync may be rescheduled automatically.

config_fields.name: wait_to_avoid_fatal_errors
config_fields.description: Deprecated - this option is no longer functional and will be removed in a future version. Rate limiting is now handled automatically by the connector.

config_fields.name: ad_statuses
config_fields.description: Select the statuses you want to be loaded in the stream. If no specific statuses are selected, the API's default behavior applies, and some statuses may be filtered out.

config_fields.name: adset_statuses
config_fields.description: Select the statuses you want to be loaded in the stream. If no specific statuses are selected, the API's default behavior applies, and some statuses may be filtered out.

config_fields.name: campaign_statuses
config_fields.description: Select the statuses you want to be loaded in the stream. If no specific statuses are selected, the API's default behavior applies, and some statuses may be filtered out.

config_fields.name: client_id
config_fields.description: The Client Id for your OAuth app

config_fields.name: client_secret
config_fields.description: The Client Secret for your OAuth app

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Credentials for connecting to the Facebook Marketing API

config_fields.name: custom_insights
config_fields.description: A list which contains ad statistics entries, each entry must have a name and can contains fields, breakdowns or action_breakdowns. Click on "add" to fill this field.

config_fields.name: default_ads_insights_action_breakdowns
config_fields.description: Action breakdowns for the Built-in Ads Insights stream that will be used in the request. You can override default values or remove them to make it empty if needed.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: The date until which you'd like to replicate data for all incremental streams, in the format YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z. All data generated between the start date and this end date will be replicated. Not setting this option will result in always syncing the latest data.

config_fields.name: fetch_thumbnail_images
config_fields.description: Set to active if you want to fetch the thumbnail_url and store the result in thumbnail_data_url for each Ad Creative.

config_fields.name: include_incrementality
config_fields.description: If enabled, the incrementality attribution window will be included in the action attribution windows for all built-in insight streams. This allows you to retrieve incrementality data for action metrics. See the Facebook Marketing API documentation for more details: https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/reference/ads-action-stats/

config_fields.name: insights_job_timeout
config_fields.description: Insights Job Timeout establishes the maximum amount of time (in minutes) of waiting for the report job to complete. When timeout is reached the job is considered failed and we are trying to request smaller amount of data by breaking the job to few smaller ones. If you definitely know that 60 minutes is not enough for your report to be processed then you can decrease the timeout value, so we start breaking job to smaller parts faster.

config_fields.name: insights_lookback_window
config_fields.description: The attribution window. Facebook freezes insight data 28 days after it was generated, which means that all data from the past 28 days may have changed since we last emitted it, so you can retrieve refreshed insights from the past by setting this parameter. If you set a custom lookback window value in Facebook account, please provide the same value here.

config_fields.name: page_size
config_fields.description: Page size used when sending requests to Facebook API to specify number of records per page when response has pagination. Most users do not need to set this field unless they specifically need to tune the connector to address specific issues or use cases.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: The date from which you'd like to replicate data for all incremental streams, in the format YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z. If not set then all data will be replicated for usual streams and only last 2 years for insight streams.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ. Only messages, threads, and drafts received on or after this date will be replicated. If not set, all historical data will be replicated.

name: GNews

config_fields.name: api_key
config_fields.description: API Key

config_fields.name: country
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to specify the country where the news articles returned by the API were published, the contents of the articles are not necessarily related to the specified country. You have to set as value the 2 letters code of the country you want to filter.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to filter the articles that have a publication date smaller than or equal to the  specified value. The date must respect the following format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss (in UTC)

config_fields.name: in
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to choose in which attributes the keywords are searched. The attributes that can be set are title, description and content. It is possible to combine several attributes.

config_fields.name: nullable
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to specify the attributes that you allow to return null values. The attributes that  can be set are title, description and content. It is possible to combine several attributes

config_fields.name: query
config_fields.description:
This parameter allows you to specify your search keywords to find the news articles you are looking for. The keywords will be used to return the most relevant articles. It is possible to use logical operators  with keywords. - Phrase Search Operator: This operator allows you to make an exact search. Keywords surrounded by 
  quotation marks are used to search for articles with the exact same keyword
sequence. 
  For example the query: "Apple iPhone" will return articles matching at
least once this sequence of keywords. - Logical AND Operator: This operator allows you to make sure that several keywords are all used in the article
  search. By default the space character acts as an AND operator, it is
possible to replace the space character 
  by AND to obtain the same result. For example the query: Apple Microsoft
is equivalent to Apple AND Microsoft - Logical OR Operator: This operator allows you to retrieve articles matching the keyword a or the keyword b.
  It is important to note that this operator has a higher precedence than
the AND operator. For example the 
  query: Apple OR Microsoft will return all articles matching the keyword
Apple as well as all articles matching 
  the keyword Microsoft
- Logical NOT Operator: This operator allows you to remove from the results the articles corresponding to the
  specified keywords. To use it, you need to add NOT in front of each word
or phrase surrounded by quotes.
  For example the query: Apple NOT iPhone will return all articles matching
the keyword Apple but not the keyword
  iPhone

config_fields.name: sortby
config_fields.description:
This parameter allows you to choose with which type of sorting the articles should be returned. Two values  are possible:
  - publishedAt = sort by publication date, the articles with the most recent
publication date are returned first
  - relevance = sort by best match to keywords, the articles with the best
match are returned first

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to filter the articles that have a publication date greater than or equal to the  specified value. The date must respect the following format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss (in UTC)

config_fields.name: top_headlines_query
config_fields.description:
This parameter allows you to specify your search keywords to find the news articles you are looking for. The keywords will be used to return the most relevant articles. It is possible to use logical operators  with keywords. - Phrase Search Operator: This operator allows you to make an exact search. Keywords surrounded by 
  quotation marks are used to search for articles with the exact same keyword
sequence. 
  For example the query: "Apple iPhone" will return articles matching at
least once this sequence of keywords. - Logical AND Operator: This operator allows you to make sure that several keywords are all used in the article
  search. By default the space character acts as an AND operator, it is
possible to replace the space character 
  by AND to obtain the same result. For example the query: Apple Microsoft
is equivalent to Apple AND Microsoft - Logical OR Operator: This operator allows you to retrieve articles matching the keyword a or the keyword b.
  It is important to note that this operator has a higher precedence than
the AND operator. For example the 
  query: Apple OR Microsoft will return all articles matching the keyword
Apple as well as all articles matching 
  the keyword Microsoft
- Logical NOT Operator: This operator allows you to remove from the results the articles corresponding to the
  specified keywords. To use it, you need to add NOT in front of each word
or phrase surrounded by quotes.
  For example the query: Apple NOT iPhone will return all articles matching
the keyword Apple but not the keyword
  iPhone

config_fields.name: convert_conversions_event
config_fields.description: Enables conversion of `conversions:*` event metrics from integers to floats. This is beneficial for preventing data rounding when the API returns float values for any `conversions:*` fields.

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Credentials for the service

config_fields.name: custom_reports_array
config_fields.description: You can add your Custom Analytics report by creating one.

config_fields.name: date_ranges_end_date
config_fields.description: The end date from which to replicate report data in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Data generated after this date will not be included in the report. Not applied to custom Cohort reports. When no date is provided or the date is in the future, the date from today is used.

config_fields.name: date_ranges_start_date
config_fields.description: The start date from which to replicate report data in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Data generated before this date will not be included in the report. Not applied to custom Cohort reports.

config_fields.name: keep_empty_rows
config_fields.description: If false, each row with all metrics equal to 0 will not be returned. If true, these rows will be returned if they are not separately removed by a filter. More information is available in the documentation.

config_fields.name: lookback_window
config_fields.description: Since attribution changes after the event date, and Google Analytics has a data processing latency, we should specify how many days in the past we should refresh the data in every run. So if you set it at 5 days, in every sync it will fetch the last bookmark date minus 5 days.

config_fields.name: property_ids
config_fields.description: A list of your Property IDs. The Property ID is a unique number assigned to each property in Google Analytics, found in your GA4 property URL. This ID allows the connector to track the specific events associated with your property. Refer to the Google Analytics documentation to locate your property ID.

config_fields.name: subscription_tier
config_fields.description: Quota tier of the Google Analytics 4 properties being queried. Determines the per-property rate-limit policy applied locally once the tier-aware rate-limit budget is activated. Select "Analytics 360 Property" only if all configured property IDs belong to an Analytics 360 subscription. See https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/reporting/data/v1/quotas.

name: Google Sheets

config_fields.name: allow_leading_numbers
config_fields.description: Allows column names to start with numbers. Example: "50th Percentile" → "50_th_percentile" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: batch_size
config_fields.description: Default value is 1000000. An integer representing row batch size for each sent request to Google Sheets API. Row batch size means how many rows are processed from the google sheet, for example default value 1000000 would process rows 2-1000002, then 1000003-2000003 and so on. Based on Google Sheets API limits documentation, it is possible to send up to 300 requests per minute, but each individual request has to be processed under 180 seconds, otherwise the request returns a timeout error. In regards to this information, consider network speed and number of columns of the google sheet when deciding a batch_size value.

config_fields.name: combine_letter_number_pairs
config_fields.description: Combines adjacent letters and numbers. Example: "Q3 2023" → "q3_2023" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: combine_number_word_pairs
config_fields.description: Combines adjacent numbers and words. Example: "50th Percentile?" → "_50th_percentile_" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Credentials for connecting to the Google Sheets API

config_fields.name: names_conversion
config_fields.description: Converts column names to a SQL-compliant format (snake_case, lowercase, etc). If enabled, you can further customize the sanitization using the options below.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: Number of concurrent threads for syncing. Higher values can speed up syncs for spreadsheets with multiple sheets, but may hit rate limits. Google Sheets API limits to 300 read requests per minute (~5 req/sec).

config_fields.name: read_empty_header_columns
config_fields.description: When enabled, the connector will continue reading columns after empty header cells and will include data from those columns using generated column names (e.g., "column_C"). By default, the connector stops reading columns when it encounters an empty header cell.

config_fields.name: remove_leading_trailing_underscores
config_fields.description: Removes leading and trailing underscores from column names. Does not remove leading underscores from column names that start with a number. Example: "50th Percentile? "→ "_50_th_percentile" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: remove_special_characters
config_fields.description: Removes all special characters from column names. Example: "Example ID*" → "example_id" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: spreadsheet_id
config_fields.description: Enter the link to the Google spreadsheet you want to sync. To copy the link, click the 'Share' button in the top-right corner of the spreadsheet, then click 'Copy link'.

config_fields.name: stream_name_overrides
config_fields.description: manual intervention needed

config_fields.name: property_history_lookback_window
config_fields.description: How far back (in minutes) to re-fetch records during incremental syncs for property history streams (deals, contacts, companies property history). Set this if you notice missing records in property history streams caused by cursor drift from HubSpot calculated properties.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated. If not set, "2006-06-01T00:00:00Z" (Hubspot creation date) will be used as start date. It's recommended to provide relevant to your data start date value to optimize synchronization.

config_fields.name: treat_numbers_and_booleans_as_strings
config_fields.description: If enabled, HubSpot dynamic `number` and `boolean` properties are exposed as `string`. Useful when HubSpot returns values that do not match the declared type and the destination rejects the records. After changing this setting, refresh the source schema and refresh the affected stream data. See the connector documentation for details.

name: Kafka

config_fields.name: MessageFormat
config_fields.description: The serialization used based on this

config_fields.name: auto_commit_interval_ms
config_fields.description: The frequency in milliseconds that the consumer offsets are auto-committed to Kafka if enable.auto.commit is set to true.

config_fields.name: auto_offset_reset
config_fields.description: What to do when there is no initial offset in Kafka or if the current offset does not exist any more on the server - earliest: automatically reset the offset to the earliest offset, latest: automatically reset the offset to the latest offset, none: throw exception to the consumer if no previous offset is found for the consumer's group, anything else: throw exception to the consumer.

config_fields.name: bootstrap_servers
config_fields.description: A list of host/port pairs to use for establishing the initial connection to the Kafka cluster. The client will make use of all servers irrespective of which servers are specified here for bootstrapping—this list only impacts the initial hosts used to discover the full set of servers. This list should be in the form host1:port1,host2:port2,.... Since these servers are just used for the initial connection to discover the full cluster membership (which may change dynamically), this list need not contain the full set of servers (you may want more than one, though, in case a server is down).

config_fields.name: client_dns_lookup
config_fields.description: Controls how the client uses DNS lookups. If set to use_all_dns_ips, connect to each returned IP address in sequence until a successful connection is established. After a disconnection, the next IP is used. Once all IPs have been used once, the client resolves the IP(s) from the hostname again. If set to resolve_canonical_bootstrap_servers_only, resolve each bootstrap address into a list of canonical names. After the bootstrap phase, this behaves the same as use_all_dns_ips. If set to default (deprecated), attempt to connect to the first IP address returned by the lookup, even if the lookup returns multiple IP addresses.

config_fields.name: client_id
config_fields.description: An ID string to pass to the server when making requests. The purpose of this is to be able to track the source of requests beyond just ip/port by allowing a logical application name to be included in server-side request logging.

config_fields.name: enable_auto_commit
config_fields.description: If true, the consumer's offset will be periodically committed in the background.

config_fields.name: group_id
config_fields.description: The Group ID is how you distinguish different consumer groups.

config_fields.name: max_poll_records
config_fields.description: The maximum number of records returned in a single call to poll(). Note, that max_poll_records does not impact the underlying fetching behavior. The consumer will cache the records from each fetch request and returns them incrementally from each poll.

config_fields.name: max_records_process
config_fields.description: The Maximum to be processed per execution

config_fields.name: polling_time
config_fields.description: Amount of time in milliseconds Kafka connector should try to poll for messages.

config_fields.name: protocol
config_fields.description: The Protocol used to communicate with brokers.

config_fields.name: receive_buffer_bytes
config_fields.description: The size of the TCP receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) to use when reading data. If the value is -1, the OS default will be used.

config_fields.name: repeated_calls
config_fields.description: The number of repeated calls to poll() if no messages were received.

config_fields.name: request_timeout_ms
config_fields.description: The configuration controls the maximum amount of time the client will wait for the response of a request. If the response is not received before the timeout elapses the client will resend the request if necessary or fail the request if retries are exhausted.

config_fields.name: retry_backoff_ms
config_fields.description: The amount of time to wait before attempting to retry a failed request to a given topic partition. This avoids repeatedly sending requests in a tight loop under some failure scenarios.

config_fields.name: subscription
config_fields.description: You can choose to manually assign a list of partitions, or subscribe to all topics matching specified pattern to get dynamically assigned partitions.

config_fields.name: test_topic
config_fields.description: manual intervention needed

config_fields.name: disable_fetching_predictive_analytics
config_fields.description: Certain streams like the profiles stream can retrieve predictive analytics data from Klaviyo's API. However, at high volume, this can lead to service availability issues on the API which can be improved by not fetching this field. WARNING: Enabling this setting will stop the  "predictive_analytics" column from being populated in your downstream destination.

config_fields.name: lookback_window
config_fields.description: The number of days to look back when syncing data in incremental mode. This helps capture any late-arriving data. Only applies to the events_detailed stream.

config_fields.name: metric_ids
config_fields.description: OPTIONAL: Comma-separated list of specific metric IDs to use for flow_series_reports and campaign_values_reports streams. If left empty, the connector will automatically fetch reports for ALL available metrics in your account. Due to Klaviyo's strict API rate limits - see [Klaviyo's API docs](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/reference/query_campaign_values), syncing all metrics can be extremely slow and may take hours to complete. RECOMMENDED: Specify only the conversion metrics you need (e.g., "RESQ6t" for Placed Order) to avoid slow syncs. Find metric IDs in your Klaviyo account under Analytics > Metrics, or use the metrics stream to list all available metrics and their IDs.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync. The performance upper boundary is based on the limit of your Klaviyo plan. More info about the rate limit plan tiers can be found on Klaviyo's API docs.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated. This field is optional - if not provided, all data will be replicated.

config_fields.name: count
config_fields.description: A positive integer, no greater than 100. If this is specified then `count` randomly  chosen images will be returned in a JSON array. Cannot be used in conjunction with  `date` or `start_date` and `end_date`.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: Indicates that end of a date range. If `start_date` is specified without an `end_date` then `end_date` defaults to the current date.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: Indicates the start of a date range. All images in the range from `start_date` to  `end_date` will be returned in a JSON array. Must be after 1995-06-16, the first day an APOD picture was posted. There are no images for tomorrow available through this API.

config_fields.name: thumbs
config_fields.description: Indicates whether the API should return a thumbnail image URL for video files. If set to True, the API returns URL of video thumbnail. If an APOD is not a video, this parameter is ignored.

A comma-seperated string of domains (eg bbc.co.uk, techcrunch.com,

config_fields.description:
A date and optional time for the newest article allowed. This should
be in ISO 8601 format.

A comma-seperated string of domains (eg bbc.co.uk, techcrunch.com,
engadget.com) to remove from the results.

config_fields.name: language

The 2-letter ISO-639-1 code of the language you want to get headlines
for. Possible options: ar de en es fr he it nl no pt ru se ud zh.

config_fields.name: search_in
config_fields.description:
Where to apply search query. Possible values are: title, description,
content.

config_fields.name: search_query
config_fields.description:
Search query. See https://newsapi.org/docs/endpoints/everything for 
information.

config_fields.name: sort_by
config_fields.description:
The order to sort the articles in. Possible options: relevancy,
popularity, publishedAt.

config_fields.name: sources
config_fields.description:
Identifiers (maximum 20) for the news sources or blogs you want
headlines from. Use the `/sources` endpoint to locate these
programmatically or look at the sources index:
https://newsapi.com/sources. Will override both country and category.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description:
A date and optional time for the oldest article allowed. This should
be in ISO 8601 format.

config_fields.name: address
config_fields.description: The full address of the property, in the format of Street, City, State, Zip. Used to retrieve data for a specific property, or together with the radius parameter to search for listings in a specific area

config_fields.name: bath_rooms
config_fields.description: The number of bathrooms, used to search for listings matching this criteria. Supports fractions to indicate partial bathrooms

config_fields.name: bedrooms
config_fields.description: The number of bedrooms, used to search for listings matching this criteria. Use 0 to indicate a studio layout

config_fields.name: city
config_fields.description: The name of the city, used to search for listings in a specific city. This parameter is case-sensitive

config_fields.name: data_type_
config_fields.description: The type of aggregate market data to return. Defaults to "All" if not provided : All , Sale , Rental

config_fields.name: days_old
config_fields.description: The maximum number of days since a property was listed on the market, with a minimum of 1 or The maximum number of days since a property was last sold, with a minimum of 1. Used to search for properties that were sold within the specified date range

config_fields.name: history_range
config_fields.description: The time range for historical record entries, in months. Defaults to 12 if not provided

config_fields.name: latitude
config_fields.description: The latitude of the search area. Use the latitude/longitude and radius parameters to search for listings in a specific area

config_fields.name: longitude
config_fields.description: The longitude of the search area. Use the latitude/longitude and radius parameters to search for listings in a specific area

config_fields.name: property_type
config_fields.description: The type of the property, used to search for listings matching this criteria : Single Family , Condo , Townhouse , Manufactured ,  Multi-Family , Apartment , Land ,

config_fields.name: radius
config_fields.description: The radius of the search area in miles, with a maximum of 100. Use in combination with the latitude/longitude or address parameters to search for listings in a specific area

config_fields.name: state
config_fields.description: The 2-character state abbreviation, used to search for listings in a specific state. This parameter is case-sensitive

config_fields.name: status
config_fields.description: The current listing status, used to search for listings matching this criteria : Active or Inactive

config_fields.name: zipcode
config_fields.description: The 5-digit zip code, used to search for listings in a specific zip code

A regular expression which tells the connector which files to replicate. All files which match this pattern will be replicated. Use | to separate multiple patterns. See this page to understand pattern syntax (GLOBSTAR and SPLIT flags are enabled). Use pattern ** to pick up all files.

config_fields.name: provider

config_fields.name: access_token
config_fields.description: Your API access token. See here. The toke is case sensitive.

config_fields.name: backend_url
config_fields.description: Your Senseforce API backend URL. This is the URL shown during the Login screen. See here for more details. (Note: Most Senseforce backend APIs have the term 'galaxy' in their ULR)

config_fields.name: dataset_id
config_fields.description: manual intervention needed

config_fields.name: slice_range
config_fields.description: The time increment used by the connector when requesting data from the Senseforce API. The bigger the value is, the less requests will be made and faster the sync will be. On the other hand, the more seldom the state is persisted and the more likely one could run into rate limites.  Furthermore, consider that large chunks of time might take a long time for the Senseforce query to return data - meaning it could take in effect longer than with more smaller time slices. If there are a lot of data per day, set this setting to 1. If there is only very little data per day, you might change the setting to 10 or more.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25. Only data with "Timestamp" after this date will be replicated. Important note: This start date must be set to the first day of where your dataset provides data.  If your dataset has data from 2020-10-10 10:21:10, set the start_date to 2020-10-10 or later

name: Serpstat

config_fields.name: api_key
config_fields.description: Serpstat API key can be found here: https://serpstat.com/users/profile/

config_fields.name: domain
config_fields.description: The domain name to get data for (ex. serpstat.com)

config_fields.name: domains
config_fields.description: The list of domains that will be used in streams that support batch operations

config_fields.name: filter_by
config_fields.description: The field name by which the results should be filtered. Filtering the results will result in fewer API credits spent. Each stream has different filtering options. See https://serpstat.com/api/ for more details.

config_fields.name: filter_value
config_fields.description: The value of the field to filter by. Each stream has different filtering options. See https://serpstat.com/api/ for more details.

config_fields.name: page_size
config_fields.description: The number of data rows per page to be returned. Each data row can contain multiple data points. The max value is 1000. Reducing the size of the page will result in fewer API credits spent.

config_fields.name: pages_to_fetch
config_fields.description: The number of pages that should be fetched. All results will be obtained if left blank. Reducing the number of pages will result in fewer API credits spent.

config_fields.name: region_id
config_fields.description: The ID of a region to get data from in the form of a two-letter country code prepended with the g_ prefix. See the list of supported region IDs here: https://serpstat.com/api/664-request-parameters-v4/.

config_fields.name: sort_by
config_fields.description: The field name by which the results should be sorted. Each stream has different sorting options. See https://serpstat.com/api/ for more details.

config_fields.name: sort_value
config_fields.description: The value of the field to sort by. Each stream has different sorting options. See https://serpstat.com/api/ for more details.

official_application_docs.title: Shopify API versioning and deprecation policy

config_fields.name: bulk_window_in_days
config_fields.description: Defines what would be a date range per single BULK Job

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: The authorization method to use to retrieve data from Shopify

config_fields.name: fetch_transactions_user_id
config_fields.description: Defines which API type (REST/BULK) to use to fetch `Transactions` data. If you are a `Shopify Plus` user, leave the default value to speed up the fetch.

config_fields.name: fulfillment_orders_include_closed
config_fields.description: If enabled, the `Fulfillment Orders` stream includes closed fulfillment orders. Shopify excludes closed orders by default.

config_fields.name: job_checkpoint_interval
config_fields.description: The threshold, after which the single BULK Job should be checkpointed (min: 15k, max: 1M)

config_fields.name: job_product_variants_include_pres_prices
config_fields.description: If enabled, the `Product Variants` stream attempts to include `Presentment prices` field (may affect the performance).

config_fields.name: job_termination_threshold
config_fields.description: The max time in seconds, after which the single BULK Job should be `CANCELED` and retried. The bigger the value the longer the BULK Job is allowed to run.

config_fields.name: lookback_window_in_days
config_fields.description: If set to a positive number, during each incremental sync the connector will re-fetch records from the past N days before the saved state. This helps capture records that may have been missed due to race conditions or late-arriving data. The default value of 0 means no lookback and preserves existing behavior.

config_fields.name: shop
config_fields.description: The name of your Shopify store found in the URL. For example, if your URL is https://NAME.myshopify.com, then the name is 'NAME'. You may also paste the full myshopify URL (e.g. https://NAME.myshopify.com) and it will be normalized automatically.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: The date you would like to replicate data from. Format: YYYY-MM-DD. Any data before this date will not be replicated.

name: Slack

config_fields.name: channel_filter
config_fields.description: A channel name list (without leading '#' char) which limit the channels from which you'd like to sync. Empty list means no filter.

config_fields.name: channel_messages_window_size
config_fields.description: The size (in days) of the date window that will be used while syncing data from the channel messages stream. A smaller window will allow for greater parallelization when syncing records, but can lead to rate limiting errors.

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Choose how to authenticate into Slack

config_fields.name: include_archived_channels
config_fields.description: Whether to include archived channels in the sync. When disabled (default), archived channels are excluded from the Slack API response, reducing the number of API calls for downstream streams such as channel_messages, threads, and channel_members. Enable this option if you need to sync data from archived channels.

config_fields.name: include_private_channels
config_fields.description: Whether to read information from private channels that the bot is already in.  If false, only public channels will be read.  If true, the bot must be manually added to private channels.

config_fields.name: join_channels
config_fields.description: Whether to join all channels or to sync data only from channels the bot is already in.  If false, you''ll need to manually add the bot to all the channels from which you''d like to sync messages.

config_fields.name: lookback_window
config_fields.description: How far into the past to look for messages in threads, default is 0 days

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated.

config_fields.name: threads_ignore_no_replies
config_fields.description: When enabled, the threads stream will skip messages that have no replies (reply_count is 0, null, or absent), reducing the number of API calls. Disabled by default to make Threads stream contain unthreaded messages in its records.

name: Stripe

config_fields.name: account_id
config_fields.description: Your Stripe account ID (starts with 'acct_', find yours here).

config_fields.name: api_retention_streams
config_fields.description: Select streams where cursor age is validated against the Stripe API 30-day event retention period. When a selected stream's cursor is older than 30 days, the connector performs a full refresh to avoid missing data. Streams not selected here will always use incremental sync regardless of cursor age.

config_fields.name: call_rate_limit
config_fields.description: The number of API calls per second that you allow connector to make. This value can not be bigger than real API call rate limit (https://stripe.com/docs/rate-limits). If not specified the default maximum is 25 calls per second for test/sandbox tokens and 100 for production tokens.

config_fields.name: client_secret
config_fields.description: Stripe API key (usually starts with 'sk_live_'; find yours here).

config_fields.name: lookback_window_days
config_fields.description: manual intervention needed

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of worker thread to use for the sync. The performance upper boundary depends on call_rate_limit setting and type of account.

config_fields.name: slice_range
config_fields.description: The time increment used by the connector when requesting data from the Stripe API. The bigger the value is, the less requests will be made and faster the sync will be. On the other hand, the more seldom the state is persisted.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Only data generated after this date will be replicated.

native_support_notes: Implemented as the built-in Stripe connector on the connsdk declarative-HTTP template.

config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to set the maximum date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the results. Results newer than the end_date will not be shown. Default is set to the current date (today) for incremental syncs.

config_fields.description: (Optional) The query (q) parameter filters the results to only those that include that search term. The q parameter supports AND, OR and NOT operators.

config_fields.name: section
config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to filter the results by a particular section. See here for a list of all sections, and here for the sections endpoint documentation.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: Use this to set the minimum date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the results. Results older than the start_date will not be shown.

config_fields.name: tag
config_fields.description: (Optional) A tag is a piece of data that is used by The Guardian to categorise content. Use this parameter to filter results by showing only the ones matching the entered tag. See here for a list of all tags, and here for the tags endpoint documentation.
